description: Lock the 2026-07-03 compressed-air correction that scopes the industrial node to Mont Cenis-era compressed-air power and pneumatic machinery rather than generic steam, construction, or regulation contexts.

checks.reason: The corrected first practical scope used water-powered compression, reservoirs, and pressure pipes at Mont Cenis.
Hydraulic water-power compression is required for the source-backed Mont Cenis compressed-air system.
The corrected first practical scope is compressor and machine-rock-drill machinery, not a generic construction practice.
Precision machine-tool practice is required for the source-backed compressor and rock-drill machinery.
Steam engines are not the direct source-backed prerequisite for the Mont Cenis compressed-air system.
Construction is a use context for pneumatic tools, not the direct enabling machinery for the first practical compressed-air system.
The 1869 Westinghouse air-brake node is scoped to the compressed-air mechanism, while safety regulation is later adoption context.
The Westinghouse air brake is a compressed-air braking mechanism.
The 1866 Whitehead torpedo used compressed-air propulsion in the first practical scope.
